title: Definition of Industrial Dispute
description: Industrial dispute means any dispute or difference between employers and employers, or between employers and workmen, or between workmen and workmen, which is connected with the employment or non-employment or the terms of employment or with the conditions of labour, of any person.
keywords: industrial dispute, labour dispute, employment, workmen, employer, conditions of labour

title: Conditions precedent to retrenchment of workmen
description: No workman employed in any industry who has been in continuous service for not less than one year under an employer shall be retrenched by that employer until the workman has been given one month's notice in writing indicating the reasons for retrenchment and the period of notice has expired, or the workman has been paid in lieu of such notice, wages for the period of the notice.
keywords: retrenchment, termination, one month notice, compensation, workman
punishment: Retrenchment compensation: 15 days wages for every completed year of service

act_name: Employees' Provident Fund and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952
title: Penalties
description: If any employer makes default in the payment of any contribution to the Fund, the Pension Fund or the Insurance Fund, or in the transfer of accumulations required to be transferred by him, or in the payment of any charges payable under any other provision of this Act, he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years.
keywords: EPF, PF default, employer contribution, provident fund, pension fund
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine up to Rs. 10,000 or both

title: Punishment for failure to pay contributions
description: If any person fails to pay any contribution which under this Act he is liable to pay, or having deducted the employee's contribution from the wages fails to pay the same to the Corporation within the time specified, he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years.
keywords: ESI, employee insurance, contribution default, employer, ESIC
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine up to Rs. 5,000

title: Payment of gratuity
description: Gratuity shall be payable to an employee on the termination of his employment after he has rendered continuous service for not less than five years, on his superannuation, or on his retirement or resignation, or on his death or disablement due to accident or disease. The gratuity payable to an employee shall be at the rate of fifteen days' wages based on the rate of wages last drawn for every completed year of service.
keywords: gratuity, retirement, resignation, 5 years service, 15 days wages
punishment: 15 days wages for every completed year of service, maximum Rs. 20 lakhs

title: Penalties for certain offences
description: Any employer who pays to any employee less than the minimum rates of wages fixed for that employee's class of work, or less than the amount due to him under the provisions of this Act, shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
keywords: minimum wages, underpayment, employer penalty, wage violation
punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

act_name: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013
title: Definition of Sexual Harassment
description: Sexual harassment includes any one or more of the following unwelcome acts or behaviour: physical contact and advances, a demand or request for sexual favours, making sexually coloured remarks, showing pornography, any other unwelcome physical, verbal or non-verbal conduct of sexual nature.
keywords: sexual harassment, workplace harassment, POSH, unwelcome conduct, women

act_name: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013
title: Constitution of Internal Complaints Committee
description: Every employer of a workplace shall constitute a Committee to be known as the Internal Complaints Committee. The Presiding Officer shall be a woman employed at a senior level at workplace from amongst the employees.
keywords: ICC, internal complaints committee, POSH, workplace, employer duty

title: Right to payment of maternity benefit
description: Subject to the provisions of this Act, every woman shall be entitled to, and her employer shall be liable for, the payment of maternity benefit at the rate of the average daily wage for the period of her actual absence, that is to say, the period immediately preceding the day of her delivery, the actual day of her delivery and any period immediately following that day.
keywords: maternity benefit, maternity leave, pregnancy, woman employee, employer liability
punishment: 26 weeks paid maternity leave for first two children, 12 weeks for third child onwards

title: Employer's liability for compensation
description: If personal injury is caused to a workman by accident arising out of and in the course of his employment, his employer shall be liable to pay compensation. The employer shall not be so liable in respect of any injury which does not result in the total or partial disablement of the workman for a period exceeding three days.
keywords: workmen compensation, accident, injury, employer liability, disability

title: Grounds for eviction of tenants
description: Notwithstanding anything contained in any other law, no suit shall be filed for eviction of a tenant from any premises except on one or more of the following grounds: non-payment of rent, subletting without permission, causing damage to premises, requirement for personal use by landlord, premises required for repairs.
keywords: rent control, eviction, tenant, landlord, non-payment, subletting

title: Documents of which registration is compulsory
description: The following documents shall be registered: instruments of gift of immovable property, other non-testamentary instruments which purport or operate to create, declare, assign, limit or extinguish any right, title or interest in immovable property of the value of one hundred rupees and upwards, non-testamentary instruments which acknowledge the receipt or payment of any consideration on account of creation or transfer of such right.
keywords: registration, property registration, immovable property, gift deed, sale deed

title: Instruments chargeable with duty
description: Subject to the provisions of this Act and the exemptions contained in Schedule I, the following instruments shall be chargeable with duty of the amount indicated in that Schedule as the proper duty therefor respectively.
keywords: stamp duty, instrument, registration, property, agreement

title: Definition of Partnership
description: Partnership is the relation between persons who have agreed to share the profits of a business carried on by all or any of them acting for all. Persons who have entered into partnership with one another are called individually partners and collectively a firm.
keywords: partnership, partners, firm, business, profit sharing

title: Punishment for fraud
description: Any person who is found to be guilty of fraud involving an amount of at least ten lakh rupees or one per cent of the turnover of the company shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to ten years and shall also be liable to fine.
keywords: corporate fraud, company fraud, directors liability, misrepresentation
punishment: Imprisonment 6 months to 10 years with fine (minimum equal to fraud amount, max 3x)

title: Declaration and payment of dividend
description: The Board of Directors of a company may declare interim dividend during any financial year or at any time during the period from closure of financial year till holding of the annual general meeting out of the surplus in the profit and loss account or out of profits of the financial year for which such interim dividend is sought to be declared.
keywords: dividend, shareholders, profit distribution, company, board of directors

title: Punishment for rape
description: Whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
keywords: rape, sexual assault, consent, woman, punishment, rigorous imprisonment
punishment: Rigorous imprisonment 10 years to life with fine

title: Punishment for rape in certain cases
description: Whoever commits rape on a woman under sixteen years of age shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and shall also be liable to fine.
keywords: rape of minor, child rape, statutory rape, POCSO, minor victim
punishment: Rigorous imprisonment 20 years to life (natural life) with fine

title: Sexual intercourse by person in authority
description: Whoever, being in a position of authority or in a fiduciary relationship, or being a public servant, or being on the management or staff of a jail, remand home or other place of custody, or being on the management or staff of hospital, takes advantage of his official position and induces or seduces any woman to have sexual intercourse with him, shall be punished.
keywords: sexual harassment, authority abuse, fiduciary, public servant, hospital staff
punishment: Rigorous imprisonment 5-10 years with fine

title: Husband or relative subjecting woman to cruelty
description: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years and shall also be liable to fine.
keywords: domestic violence, cruelty, husband, dowry harassment, married woman
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years with fine

title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult modesty of a woman
description: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any words, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word or sound shall be heard, or that such gesture or object shall be seen, by such woman, or intrudes upon the privacy of such woman, shall be punished.
keywords: eve teasing, modesty, insult, harassment, woman, privacy intrusion
punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years with fine

title: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than one year but which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.
keywords: assault, criminal force, outrage modesty, woman, molestation
punishment: Imprisonment 1-5 years with fine

title: Criminal intimidation
description: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm to that person, or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do, or to omit to do any act which that person is legally entitled to do, commits criminal intimidation.
keywords: threat, intimidation, blackmail, extortion, coercion, alarm
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace
description: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence, shall be punished.
keywords: insult, provocation, breach of peace, public disturbance
punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

title: Devolution of interest in coparcenary property
description: On and from the commencement of the Hindu Succession (Amendment) Act, 2005, in a Joint Hindu family governed by the Mitakshara law, the daughter of a coparcener shall by birth become a coparcener in her own right in the same manner as the son and have the same rights in the coparcenary property as she would have had if she had been a son.
keywords: Hindu succession, daughter rights, coparcenary, ancestral property, inheritance

title: General rules of succession in case of males
description: The property of a male Hindu dying intestate shall devolve according to the provisions of this Chapter firstly upon the heirs, being the relatives specified in class I of the Schedule; secondly, if there is no heir of class I, then upon the heirs, being the relatives specified in class II of the Schedule.
keywords: intestate succession, male Hindu, heirs, class I heirs, inheritance

title: Application of Personal law to Muslims
description: Notwithstanding any custom or usage to the contrary, in all questions regarding intestate succession, special property of females, marriage, dissolution of marriage including talaq, ila, zihar, lian, khula and mubaraat, maintenance, dower, guardianship, gifts, trusts and trust properties, and wakfs the rule of decision shall be the Muslim Personal Law (Shariat).
keywords: Muslim law, shariat, marriage, divorce, talaq, maintenance, inheritance

title: Execution of unprivileged Wills
description: Every testator, not being a soldier employed in an expedition or engaged in actual warfare, or an airman so employed or engaged, or a mariner at sea, shall execute his Will according to the following rules: The testator shall sign or shall affix his mark to the Will, or it shall be signed by some other person in his presence and by his direction.
keywords: will, testament, execution, signature, witness, testator

act_name: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007
title: Maintenance of parents and senior citizens
description: A senior citizen including parent who is unable to maintain himself from his own earning or property owned by him, shall be entitled to make an application for maintenance. Children or relatives shall maintain such parents or senior citizens.
keywords: senior citizen, parent maintenance, old age, children duty, elderly care

act_name: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015
title: Presumption and determination of age
description: Where it appears that a person who has committed an offence is a child, the Juvenile Justice Board or a committee or a court shall make an inquiry to determine the age of that person and shall determine the age of the person as on the date of commission of the offence.
keywords: juvenile, minor, age determination, child offender, JJ Act

act_name: Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985
title: Punishment for contravention in relation to cannabis plant and cannabis
description: Whoever, in contravention of any provision of this Act or any rule or order made or condition of licence granted thereunder, produces, manufactures, possesses, sells, purchases, transports, imports inter-State, exports inter-State or uses cannabis, shall be punishable.
keywords: NDPS, cannabis, ganja, drugs, narcotic, possession, trafficking
punishment: Small quantity: up to 1 year or fine or both. Commercial quantity: 10-20 years with fine

act_name: Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985
title: Punishment for contravention in relation to manufactured drugs and preparations
description: Whoever, in contravention of any provision of this Act or any rule or order made or condition of licence granted thereunder, manufactures, possesses, sells, purchases, transports, imports inter-State, exports inter-State or uses any manufactured drug or any preparation containing any manufactured drug shall be punishable.
keywords: NDPS, manufactured drugs, heroin, cocaine, possession, trafficking
punishment: Small quantity: up to 1 year or fine or both. Commercial quantity: 10-20 years with fine Rs 1-2 lakhs

title: Punishment for certain offences
description: Whoever acquires, has in his possession or carries any firearm or ammunition in contravention of section 3, or manufactures, sells, transfers, converts, repairs, tests or proves, or exposes or offers for sale any firearm or ammunition in contravention of section 5, shall be punishable.
keywords: arms, firearms, ammunition, illegal possession, license, weapon
punishment: Imprisonment 3-7 years with fine for prohibited arms; 1-3 years for other violations

title: Punishment for being member of terrorist gang or organisation
description: Any person who is a member of a terrorist gang or a terrorist organisation, which is involved in terrorist act, shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
keywords: UAPA, terrorism, terrorist organization, member, national security
punishment: Imprisonment up to life with fine

title: Power to make orders detaining certain persons
description: The Central Government or the State Government may make an order directing that any person be detained if the Central Government or the State Government is satisfied with respect to any person that with a view to preventing him from acting in any manner prejudicial to the defence of India, the relations of India with foreign powers, or the security of India.
keywords: NSA, preventive detention, national security, defence of India
punishment: Preventive detention up to 12 months
